name: dual_narrative_leak_advisory
description:
Pins DUAL_NARRATIVE_MISLABEL via the leak-detection branch (engine/validator.py:550-565) — the ADVISORY rejection class that fires on the LLM-accept path WITHOUT triggering retry or abstain. Validator.validate_dual_narratives runs once column Q has already passed validate(); it walks _PROVIDER_ONLY_PHRASES (validator.py:136-150) against the on-prem half and _ONPREM_ONLY_PHRASES (validator.py:156-165) against the cloud half. Here `narrative_on_prem` contains 'inherited from AWS' verbatim — the first phrase in _PROVIDER_ONLY_PHRASES — so the on-prem leak branch fires (validator.py:550-557), appends a NOTE, and adds RejectionReason.DUAL_NARRATIVE_MISLABEL to dual_result.flagged.

No CRM context is supplied (assessor.py:1222-1229 sets crm_resp_for_check=None when crm_entry is None) so the CRM cross-check branch at validator.py:570-597 is skipped entirely — this case isolates the phrase-table detector from the responsibility-mismatch detector. Cloud half is populated with a clean provider-attribution paragraph that contains no _ONPREM_ONLY_PHRASES tokens (no 'physical data center', 'rack-mounted', etc.), so the symmetric cloud-side leak branch (validator.py:558-565) stays silent — only one flag should accumulate.

Column Q narrative ('Examined the program inheritance...') is independently valid: contains affirming phrases 'Examined ' and 'confirmed via' (_AFFIRMING_PHRASES at validator.py:43-60) so classify_narrative returns COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING and status=Compliant passes the expected_status check at :355; contains 'inherited from the FedRAMP-authorized cloud service provider AWS GovCloud, documented in' which satisfies _INHERITANCE_SOURCE_RE at validator.py:697-701 (whitespace + source token after 'from'), so MISSING_INHERITANCE_MARKER doesn't cascade; shares <70% Jaccard with the AC-2 `procedures` text, so REQUIREMENT_RESTATEMENT doesn't fire either.

Asserts via the new `dual_narrative_flags_contains` runner hook (test_eval_harness.py) — NOT via rejection_classes_contains, because the advisory layer routes its flags into decision.dual_narrative_flags rather than decision.rejection_log (the LLM-accept path has empty rejection_log by construction). source='llm' and needs_review=false pin the load-bearing invariant: advisory flags don't propagate to the retry budget or the human-review queue — they're a UI-fidelity signal mirrored to Assessment.dual_narrative_flag_reasons by routes/controls.py for the column-Q-split renderer.

ccis_row.guidance: The organization defines and documents the account types permitted within the information system.
ccis_row.procedures: Examine documented account management plan; confirm enumerated account types approved by designated approval authority.

tagged_evidence:
## Tagged evidence
- USD00099999 Cloud Account Management Plan Rev C — references FedRAMP-authorized provider for account-type baselines.

llm_stub_proposals.narrative: Examined the program inheritance documentation USD00099999; confirmed via the documented authorization package that account-type controls are inherited from the FedRAMP-authorized cloud service provider AWS GovCloud, documented in section 4 of the plan.
llm_stub_proposals.narrative_on_prem: Customer manages local privileged-account enrollment and quarterly recertification per the program's account management plan; the underlying directory service is inherited from AWS Directory Service in the provider boundary.
llm_stub_proposals.narrative_cloud: AWS GovCloud Directory Service provides the IAM control plane that enforces account-type baselines for the customer-managed tenant; documented in the FedRAMP-authorized provider package.
